description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with the default image debian:bookworm-slim and stages deploy and production. In the deploy stage add a Jekyll Post filename job that loops over _posts/*.* checking each filename against the YYYY-MM-DD‑slug pattern, a Vagrantfile job that installs kmod procps ruby vagrant libvirt, validates the Vagrantfile with ruby -c and vagrant validate, a YAML Front Matter job that installs python3‑yaml and yamllint then extracts front‑matter from _docs, _pages and _posts files and validates it with yamllint and a custom script, a markdownlint job (image debian:trixie-slim) that installs git and markdownlint and runs mdl on changed *.md files in a merge request, a pages job (image debian:bookworm) that runs the shared APT setup, installs curl python3‑colorama python3‑yaml, triggers spell‑check, adjusts Jekyll config, builds the site with jekyll, runs a suite of custom checks, runs linkchecker and publishes the public/ directory as a always‑on artifact, a rsync mirrors job that installs rsync and lists remote mirrors, and a shellcheck job (image debian:bookworm-slim) with appropriate MR rules. Also add a check_fdroid_apk_bot job that runs on schedule or on the master branch of fdroid/fdroidserver using alpine:3.16, installs gnupg and curl, downloads an APK and its ASC file, verifies the signature, and on failure stores the apk and asc as artifacts for 180 days. Finally add an f-droid.org job that runs manually on the fdroid/fdroidserver project using debian:bookworm, performs the shared APT setup, installs Jekyll and its Ruby dependencies via bundle, rewrites _config.yml, builds the site to $CI_PROJECT_DIR/_site, prepares multilingual assets and deploys them in the production stage.
